page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-2 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include bundled local storage or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data reveals a divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. The AMD EPYC 7B13 processor demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, ranking in the top 25%, and average single-core performance in Geekbench and text processing workloads. However, multi-core performance is weak, falling into the bottom 25% for stress-ng and the bottom 10% for Geekbench multi-core, software compilation, and ray tracing. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with read-only large block bandwidth at 26.64 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 20.4 GB/sec, both ranking in the bottom 25%.

Given its resource profile, the instance exhibits poor performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving, all ranking in the bottom 10%. However, it maintains average performance for single-threaded tasks like gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and text generation using small 135M LLM models. This makes the n2d-standard-2 suitable for lightweight general-purpose workloads, single-threaded utility scripts, basic text processing, and low-traffic web serving where high multi-core throughput and massive memory bandwidth are not required.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-2 is a general-purpose virtual machine configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, running on an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. This instance features 1 physical core with hyper-threading, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core, but lacks local storage and GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests, alongside average performance in single-threaded tasks such as gzip compression and text processing. Conversely, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are weak, with database operations, software compilation, and static web serving throughput falling into the bottom 10% to 25% ranges. Consequently, this server is best suited for lightweight general-purpose applications, single-threaded utility tasks, and basic text processing workloads rather than memory-intensive or highly parallelized database systems.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-2 is a general-purpose VM with 2 vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU. It offers strong single-core performance but limited multi-core and memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-2 is a general-purpose VM featuring 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor for lightweight workloads.
tagline: A dual-vCPU general-purpose instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing, optimized for single-threaded utility tasks and lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8 GB RAM
Strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% of stress-ng benchmarks
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Constrained multi-core performance and memory bandwidth under heavy parallel workloads
Best suited for lightweight general-purpose applications, text processing, and single-threaded utilities
